drifted.spec_text: The framework MUST produce compile-time errors when macro usage is incorrect
drifted.actual: Compile-time errors exist but no trybuild/compile-fail tests validate them

drifted.spec_text: The framework MUST provide a prelude module that re-exports the most commonly used types
drifted.actual: Prelude module missing query_interface! macro and Receptacle from explicit re-exports

drifted.spec_text: 100% of public APIs have passing doc tests and unit tests
drifted.actual: Most public APIs have doc tests but gaps remain (InterfaceMap::info(), prelude doc test is inert)

drifted.spec_text: All Criterion benchmarks demonstrate no regressions between releases
drifted.actual: Benchmarks exist but no CI regression gate (cargo bench not in CI pipeline)

drifted.spec_text: Factory functions MUST return components with an initial reference count of 1
drifted.actual: Macro-generated components have higher initial ref count due to internal self-referential Arcs in interface map; tests use base_count workaround

drifted.spec_text: The registry MUST provide a simplified factory registration method (register_simple)
drifted.actual: register_simple is implemented with doc-test but has no integration test in tests/registry.rs

drifted.spec_text: Actor components MUST use the same component, interface, and receptacle model as plain components (defined via the existing macro system)
drifted.actual: Actor<M,H> is hand-coded with manual IUnknown impl, bypassing define_component! macro

drifted.spec_text: Actor and channel components MUST be registerable in the existing component registry
drifted.actual: Only actor registration tested; channel components never registered as named factories in registry

drifted.spec_text: Actor and channel components MUST support both first-party and third-party binding
drifted.actual: Third-party binding (string-name bind()) never tested for actor-to-channel wiring; all examples use first-party only

specs.title: NUMA-Aware Actor Thread Pinning and Memory Allocation

drifted.spec_text: The framework MUST validate CPU IDs against the system's available CPUs and return an error for invalid IDs before spawning the thread
drifted.actual: validate_cpus() function exists at cpuset.rs:355 but is NOT called in Actor::activate(); invalid CPUs produce generic kernel errors instead of structured CpuOffline(id) errors

drifted.spec_text: Channel buffers SHOULD be allocated on the NUMA node associated with the actor's CPU affinity
drifted.actual: SpscChannel::new_numa and MpscChannel::new_numa accept node parameter but discard it, delegating to Self::new(capacity); NumaAllocator not used for ring buffer allocation
